title: Israel says Ali Larijani, top Iranian security official, killed by Israel in strike - AP News
description: Israel's defense minister says Iran’s top security official and the head of the Revolutionary Guard’s Basij militia were both killed in overnight strikes in a blow to the country’s leadership. Both security official Ali Larijani and Gen. Gholam Reza Soleimani…

title: Illinois Senate primary race is test of Pritzker's sway, Democrats' views of ICE, outside spending - CBS News
description: The 2026 Democratic primary campaign for retiring Sen. Dick Durbin's Senate seat is largely a three-person race. Voters go to the polls Tuesday in Illinois.
content: Illinois Senate primary race is test of Pritzker's sway, Democrats' views of ICE, outside spending - CBS News. The 2026 Democratic primary campaign for retiring Sen. Dick Durbin's Senate seat is largely a three-person race. Voters go to the polls Tuesday in Illinois.

title: Iranian women soccer players granted asylum in Australia are pictured training in Brisbane - AP News
description: Two Iranian women soccer players have been seen training with the Brisbane Roar after Australia granted them asylum, and they stayed behind as teammates headed home. On Monday the club posted photos of Fatemeh Pasandideh and Atefeh Ramezanisadeh at a session …

title: Bombs explode in northeastern Nigeria, leaving scores killed and injured, authorities say - AP News
description: Bomb blasts hit Maiduguri in northeastern Nigeria’s Borno state, killing and injuring many people, according to emergency officials. Officials say the explosions happened on Monday night in at least three places. Nigeria’s emergency agency says blasts hit the…

title: UAE Holds 19 Indians Over Fake Social Media Posts - Gulte
description: Over 35 social media influencers, including 19 Indians, were arrested by the UAE authorities for publishing video clips that contained fabricated content on the Middle East tension, which could incite the public and create disorder. UAE official news agency W…

title: ‘Nobody told us it was dangerous’: Quartz countertop boom linked to incurable lung disease among Bay Area workers - The Mercury News
description: A surge in quartz countertop fabrication has triggered a growing epidemic of silicosis — an incurable lung disease — among Bay Area workers who cut and polish the popular kitchen material.
content: ‘Nobody told us it was dangerous’: Quartz countertop boom linked to incurable lung disease among Bay Area workers - The Mercury News. A surge in quartz countertop fabrication has triggered a growing epidemic of silicosis — an incurable lung disease — among Bay Area workers who cut and polish the popular kitchen material.
